close_reason: Implemented versioned registry with lib helpers (mkRegistryOverlay, mkMetaPackage, resolveTool), converted embedded registry to versioned format, moved generators to internal packages
description:
Extend the toolchain registry to support multiple versions of each toolchain.

## Current State
- Registry maps toolchain name to a single package (e.g., `go = pkgs.go`)
- No way to request a specific version (e.g., Go 1.21 vs 1.22)

## Desired State
- Support versioned toolchain requests in toolchain.toml:
  ```toml
  [toolchains]
  go = { version = "1.22" }
  rust = { version = "1.75" }
  python = { version = "3.12" }
  nodejs = { version = "20" }
  typescript = { version = "5.3" }
  ```
- Default to latest stable if no version specified
- Registry provides version resolution logic

## Languages to Support
- **Go**: go_1_21, go_1_22, go_1_23, etc.
- **Rust**: Multiple rustc/cargo versions
- **Python**: python310, python311, python312, python313
- **Node.js**: nodejs_18, nodejs_20, nodejs_22
- **TypeScript**: Different tsc versions

## Implementation Ideas
- Use nixpkgs version suffixed packages where available
- Consider rust-overlay for Rust toolchains
- Consider python-flake for Python versions
- May need custom overlay for some toolchains

## Considerations
- How to handle toolchain-specific features (e.g., Rust editions)
- Cross-version compatibility in monorepo (all code uses same version)
- Migration path when bumping versions
title: Implement versioned toolchain registry
